description: Direct questions posed to audience about preferences, experiences, or opinions on food/beverage topics, designed to prompt comments and community discussion.
structural_recipe: End caption with a clear, relatable question about taste preferences, preparation methods, or personal experiences. Use emoji to emphasize the question. Position question at end of narrative or storytelling content. Employ Arabic phrasing that feels conversational (وين، أي، صح؟). Can be single direct question or double question format.
why_it_works: Saudi audiences value personal opinion-sharing and collective experience. Questions invite dialogue in a culture where community validation and shared taste preferences matter. Direct address (أنت) creates intimacy. Food/beverage choices are identity markers in Saudi culture, making preference questions highly personal and comment-worthy.
transplantation_caveats: Requires authentic Arabic phrasing for Saudi market; direct English translations reduce effectiveness
Questions should reference culturally relevant food contexts (steak preparation, coffee tradition, dining establishments)
Avoid questions that feel generic or imported from Western content; localization essential
Best paired with visual content showing the food/beverage item discussed
cultural_constraints: Questions about alcohol are inappropriate; focus on halal-certified foods and traditional beverages
Preference questions work best when tied to dining culture, craftsmanship, or family traditions
Timing: questions perform better in evening/social hours when audiences engage with food content
